Title: About 50 principal, headmaster posts filled - Fadhlina
Article:
IPOH: The Education Ministry has filled about 50 vacant headmaster and principal posts nationwide in a move aimed at strengthening school administration and addressing urgent operational and safety concerns.
Its minister Fadhlina Sidek said schools cannot function effectively without strong leadership, stressing that principals and headmasters are essential in building a stable school ecosystem.
She said the vacancies were identified as part of ongoing efforts to reform school administration and improve daily management, particularly in relation to safety and governance issues.
"That is why vacancies in principal posts at educational institutions cannot be taken lightly, as this has a direct impact on the smooth administration of schools as well as the continuity of educational leadership.
"To address this matter, we have decided that in line with improvements to governance and administrative management, the filling of vacant principal posts in several educational institutions will begin effective today," she said.
She told reporters this after attending the 55th national-level Teachers' Day Awards ceremony held at a hotel here. Also present was her deputy, Wong Kah Woh.
Fadhlina added that the ministry had instructed senior officials to urgently review and resolve the matter, describing it as a priority requiring immediate attention.
"Therefore, I have asked the ministry's director-general and the chief secretary to examine our commitment to resolving these issues quickly," she said.
She said the ministry has now completed the appointment process, with all newly appointed principals and headmasters presented during a ceremony.
"Today, we have resolved all vacancy issues by appointing headmasters and principals so that school administration can continue smoothly," she said.
Fadhlina said the appointments take effect immediately, allowing the officers to assume their duties at their respective schools.
She added that while about 50 posts were initially vacant, the ministry would continue to monitor and fill any remaining gaps promptly.
"There are still vacancies, and we will ensure they are filled from time to time as quickly as possible," she said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Sunway issues tranche 1 series 1 Sukuk Wakalah worth US$5 mil
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Sunway Bhd has issued its first issuance of Sukuk Wakalah (Tranche 1 Series 1 Sukuk Wakalah) amounting to US$5 million (US$1=RM3.95).
In a filing with Bursa Malaysia, it said proceeds raised from the issuance of Tranche 1 Series 1 Sukuk Wakalah shall be utilised to finance Shariah-compliant investment activities, capital expenditure, working capital requirements and/or other general corporate purposes of Sunway Group.
The issuance of Tranche 1 Series 1 Sukuk Wakalah is under the Multi-Currency Islamic Medium Term Notes programme of up to RM10 billion in nominal value (or its equivalent in other currencies) based on the Shariah Principle of Wakalah Bi Al-Istithmar by Sunway Treasury Sdn Bhd.
The scheduled dissolution date or maturity date is May 18, 2026, while the tenure is one year and three days.
HSBC Amanah Malaysia Bhd is the sole lead manager for the issuance of Tranche 1 Series 1 Sukuk Wakalah.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Carlsberg net profit rises to RM98mil, declares 24 sen dividend in Q1
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Carlsberg Brewery Malaysia Bhd's net profit rose 4.7 per cent to RM98.94 million in the first quarter ended March 31, 2026 (Q1 FY26), from RM94.52 million a year earlier, on the back of higher revenue.
Quarterly revenue rose 6.5 per cent to RM705.95 million from RM662.81 million previously, the company's filing to Bursa Malaysia showed.
The brewer said the improved earnings were contributed by a longer selling period ahead of Chinese New Year this year in both Malaysia and Singapore.
Earnings per share came in higher at 32.36 sen compared to 30.91 sen a year ago.
According to Carlsberg, its Malaysia operations delivered revenue growth of 9.6 per cent and a 2.9 per cent increase in profit from operations, driven by festive demand and effective market execution.
Singapore operations recorded a three per cent increase in total revenue in local currency, resulting in higher profit from operations driven by lower operating spend.
However, reported revenue declined due to the appreciation of the ringgit against the Singapore dollar.
Carlsberg declared a first interim dividend of 24 sen per share for the quarter ended March 31, 2026.
Commenting on the results, Carlsberg Malaysia managing director Stefano Clini said the group delivered a commendable first quarter on favourable Chinese New Year timing, despite heightened global uncertainty and subdued consumer spending.
"Anchored by our strategic priorities and disciplined execution, we remain resilient and agile in responding to evolving market dynamics across Malaysia and Singapore, while continuing to deliver value to consumers and stakeholders," he said in a statement.
Clini added that the company logged strong single‑digit growth across its mainstream and premium beers, supported by consumer promotions across both on- and off-trade channels as well as e-commerce during the festive period.
This was further bolstered by the launch of Somersby Shandy in Malaysia and Singapore, with the majority of retail outlets selling out within weeks of its release.
On prospects, Clini said Carlsberg  remains cautious amid geopolitical tensions, energy and input cost volatility and broader macroeconomic uncertainty.
He said that against this backdrop, the company will prioritise disciplined value management, cost optimisation and prudent resource allocation, while continuing to innovate and invest in its brands, brewery capabilities and digital transformation initiatives.
"In addition, we are proactively monitoring and managing any potential supply chain risk related to Middle East tensions to ensure operational continuity.
"Through consistent execution and financial discipline, we aim to strengthen resilience and reinforce our commitment to long-term sustainable value creation," he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Case closed: MACC classifies IJM chairman probe as NFA
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has classified investigations involving IJM Corporation Bhd non-executive chairman Tan Sri Krishnan Tan as no further action (NFA).
In a filing with Bursa Malaysia, the company said the anti-graft agency had confirmed that no prosecution would be pursued in relation to investigations linked to the voluntary takeover offer by Sunway Bhd.
"The company has cooperated fully with MACC throughout the process and remained committed to supporting the authorities as required," IJM said.
IJM reiterated that, as announced on March 24, MACC had confirmed the investigation did not involve the company as an entity.
In a separate statement, Tan welcomed the decision, saying he had "steadfastly maintained innocence".
He said the investigations and related publicity had caused severe reputational damage, undermining what he described as a lifetime of public and corporate service.
"It caused immense strain on my family, friends, and the organisations I serve - to all of whom I am grateful, especially my wife and family and dear friends, for standing by me. I now want to put this difficult period behind me and move on," he said.
IJM shares closed unchanged at RM2.18, giving the company a market capitalisation of RM7.95 billion.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 80pct of teacher transfer cases involving spouses resolved - Fadhlina
Article:
IPOH: Teacher welfare, particularly involving long-distance postings and transfer applications, continues to be a priority for the Education Ministry.
Minister Fadhlina Sidek said the government remains sensitive to the challenges faced by teachers nationwide, especially those separated from their spouses and families due to work postings.
"I believe it is important for us to look at what the ministry has done to address and manage these issues. We are very empathetic and take into account all the circumstances faced by our teachers.
"Because of that, if we look at the measures that have been taken to ensure that teachers' welfare issues such as long-distance postings are addressed, among them is reducing the duration required for transfer applications.
"Previously, the transfer process took quite a long time. We have now provided some flexibility, where applications can be considered after two years.
"Secondly, in the context of long-distance spouse postings, the ministry gives priority to these cases," she told reporters after attending the 55th national-level Teachers' Day Awards ceremony held at a hotel here.
Also present was her deputy, Wong Kah Woh.
She added that cases involving long-distance spouse postings make up the majority of transfer applications handled by the ministry.
She said about 80 per cent of transfer applications involve long-distance spouse cases, and the ministry managed to resolve these.
"The remainder involve health and safety-related issues, which are also given priority consideration," she said.
She said the ministry takes all applications seriously and ensures that teacher welfare remains at the centre of its policy decisions.
"We understand the situation and take all applications into account. The objective is to safeguard the welfare of our teachers," she said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Sunway REIT appoints Derek Teh as CEO effective Dec 1, 2025
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Sunway Real Estate Investment Trust (Sunway REIT) has appointed Derek Teh Wan Wei as chief executive officer (CEO) of Sunway REIT Management Sdn Bhd, effective Dec 1, 2025.
In a Bursa Malaysia filing today, Sunway REIT said Teh, 48, currently serves as Malaysian REIT Managers Association's vice-chairman. Before joining Sunway REIT Management, he was Sentral REIT Management Sdn Bhd CEO.
"He brings over 20 years of experience in the property industry. He previously held senior positions across several organisations, including Malaysian Resources Corporation Bhd's vice-president for properties.
"He also served at CIMB-Mapletree Management Sdn Bhd, where he led investment, fund management, development management and asset management functions for a portfolio of real estate assets, culminating as chief investment officer," it said.
Teh holds two bachelor's degrees in Property and Construction, and Planning and Design from the University of Melbourne, and a Master of Business Systems from Monash University.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Petaling Jaya activates flood sirens as Sungai Penchala water level rises
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Flood warning sirens were activated in several areas around Petaling Jaya today after continuous heavy rain caused water levels at Sungai Penchala to rise to dangerous levels.
The Petaling Jaya City Council (MBPJ), in an advisory issued this evening on Facebook, said the river's water level had reached the danger threshold and could continue to rise if the downpour persisted.
As a precautionary measure, the flood warning system was activated to alert residents living near affected areas.
In the post, MBPJ said its rapid response unit, Skuad Pantas MBPJ, had also been deployed to monitor the situation and carry out immediate response measures from time to time.
Residents are advised to avoid going near rivers and large drains due to strong currents and to remain cautious while driving during bad weather conditions.
"The public is also advised to comply with instructions issued by the authorities from time to time," the statement said.
The latest warning comes amid growing concern over flash floods in Petaling Jaya following several recent incidents caused by prolonged heavy rain.
Earlier this month, several areas in Petaling Jaya and nearby suburbs experienced flash floods after hours of torrential rain, resulting in inundated roads, stranded vehicles and water entering homes in low-lying residential areas.
Among the areas previously affected were Sections 13 and 17, Kota Damansara, Kampung Tunku and parts of Damansara Perdana, with residents sharing videos and photographs of rising floodwaters on social media.
Local authorities and emergency responders have since intensified monitoring at flood-prone locations, especially during periods of continuous rainfall.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Police probe Bedong school fight involving female students
Article:
SUNGAI PETANI: Police have identified between six and 10 female students believed to be involved in a fight in front of a secondary school in Bedong, which went viral on social media since yesterday.
Kuala Muda district police chief ACP Hanyan Ramlan said the incident involved secondary school students from Form One to Form Four, but initial investigations found that only two or three students were directly involved in the fight.
He said the others were believed to have been trying to defuse the situation, causing it to appear as though the incident involved a large group.
"Our review of the viral video found that the actions of several students attempting to break up the fight created the impression that everyone at the scene was involved in the incident," he told reporters here, today.
According to him, police are reviewing several other video recordings to identify other individuals at the scene during the incident and they would be called to the police station to have their statements recorded to facilitate investigations.
Hanyan said no arrests or remand have been made against any individual so far, while no weapons were used or serious injuries were reported in the incident..
"Police have opened seven investigation papers related to the case, including investigations into the actual cause of the fight, which is believed to have stemmed from a misunderstanding on social media among several students," he said.
He also advised the public not to speculate on the incident and to allow investigations to proceed, while hoping his explanation would help ease concerns following the viral video. – BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar: Move to revoke heritage players' citizenship must follow due process
Article:
SEREMBAN: Any move to revoke the citizenship of the seven suspended Harimau Malaya heritage players must go through the proper process, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said today.
Anwar said the matter should first be discussed by the Football Association of Malaysia (FAM) before being referred to the Home Ministry for further consideration.
"Let the football association discuss it first, then present it to the home minister, following the normal process," he told reporters after officiating the national-level 2026 National Youth Day celebration here today.
Anwar was responding to questions on proposals to revoke the citizenship of the seven suspended Harimau Malaya heritage players.
Earlier, FAM suffered a major blow when seven heritage players who joined the Harimau Malaya squad in 2025 were suspended for 12 months after being found to be involved in issues related to document forgery.
In September 2025, the International Federation of Association Football (
FIFA
) ruled that there were elements of alteration or falsification involving the players' eligibility documents.
The seven players are Gabriel Palmero, Facundo Garces, Rodrigo Holgado, Imanol Machuca, Joao Figueiredo, Jon Irazabal and Hector Hevel.
Fifa had earlier imposed a CHF350,000 (about RM1.8 million) fine on FAM, while each of the seven heritage players was fined CHF2,000 (RM10,000) and suspended for 12 months.
FAM subsequently filed an appeal with the Court of Arbitration for Sport (CAS) in October 2025 to challenge the decision.
However, in March this year, CAS upheld Fifa's ruling, although it granted some leniency by allowing the players to continue training and remain with their respective clubs, but barred them from participating in official matches throughout the suspension period.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bursa Malaysia ends lower, tracking weak regional sentiment
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia ended the day on a low note, in line with the weaker regional market sentiment as investors adopted a cautious stance amid renewed concerns over rising global oil prices and potential inflationary spillover across major economies.
At 5pm, the benchmark FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) slipped by 5.36 points, or 0.30 per cent, to 1,740.22 from Thursday's close of 1,745.58.
The benchmark index, which opened 2.11 points higher at 1,747.69, moved between 1,735.51 and 1,748.85 throughout the day.
Market breadth was negative with losers surpassing gainers 814 to 427, while 581 counters were unchanged, 906 untraded, and 47 suspended.
Turnover was marginally higher at 3.92 billion units worth RM3.46 billion compared with 3.91 billion units worth RM3.22 billion on Thursday.
IPPFA Sdn Bhd director of investment strategy and country economist, Mohd Sedek Jantan said Brent crude oil futures climbed nearly seven per cent this week amid the lack of progress in reopening the Strait of Hormuz, although prices remain below the key US$110 per barrel threshold.
He said sentiment was further influenced by United States (US) President Donald Trump's comments that China was looking to purchase more US oil, reinforcing expectations of tighter near-term energy market conditions.
"On the domestic front, selling pressure was concentrated in petroleum-related counters as investors reassessed margin outlooks and the broader impact of elevated energy prices on inflation expectations and operating costs," he told Bernama.
Echoing the statement, Rakuten Trade Sdn Bhd vice-president of equity research, Thong Pak Leng said investors' optimism over technology stocks faded amid rising inflation concerns, which pushed US Treasury yields to one-year highs and increased expectations of another rate hike this year.
"As for the local bourse, we maintain our cautious stance, given the heightened volatility and uncertainty surrounding global interest rates, geopolitical developments and external market conditions.
"The recent pullback in the FBM KLCI reflects softer risk appetite, although the absence of aggressive selling suggests that underlying support in the market remains relatively intact," he added.
Among heavyweights, Maybank fell eight sen to RM11.10 and CIMB lost two sen to RM7.88, while Public Bank added eight sen to RM4.91, Tenaga Nasional rose six sen to RM14.54 and IHH Healthcare gained two sen to RM9.04.
On the most active list, AirAsia X slipped seven sen to RM1.15, Top Glove decreased five sen to 78 sen and Genetec eased half a sen to 32.5 sen, while IRIS jumped 9.5 sen to 27.5 sen and GIIB was one sen firmer at 29 sen.
Among the top gainers, MKH jumped 33 sen to RM1.46, Fraser & Neave advanced 30 sen to RM29.0, United Plantations was 28 sen firmer at RM30.26 and Lianson widened 23 sen to RM1.78.
As for the top losers, Nestle slid RM2.20 to RM100.20, Petronas Dagangan contracted RM1.08 to RM20.30, Kuala Lumpur Kepong fell 56 sen to RM20.32 and Hong Leong Industries eased 46 sen to RM18.34.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: #NSTviral: Japanese tourist's bus experience raises questions on Visit Malaysia campaign
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A viral video showing a Japanese tourist being called out by a bus driver after allegedly failing to pay her fare has sparked debate online over the friendliness of Malaysia's public transport system towards foreign visitors ahead of Visit Malaysia Year.
The 46-second video, posted on X by user @kamaghul, showed a Japanese woman vlogging her journey while attempting to board a Rapid KL bus.
In the video, the bus driver told the tourist that she had not paid before boarding the bus.
The tourist then appeared confused and attempted to scan a QR code using her mobile phone, but no QR payment option was available.
She later asked whether she could pay using cash but was informed that only Touch 'n Go and RapidKL cards were accepted.
The clip quickly gained traction online, with many netizens debating whether Malaysia's public transport payment system was convenient for tourists.
Some users also criticised the driver's tone, saying the situation could have been handled more politely.
X user @quaeenconberse wrote: "But does the driver need to be that rude? I did my research when travelling too but sometimes there are other transport systems not listed. That's normal when travelling, things happen."
Another user, @UntoldHero, said Malaysia's public transport system was still not tourist-friendly.
"If you Google it, tourists still would not know what card to buy if they never use public transport here. Touch 'n Go cards are also not easy to find.
"They are not being entitled, they just do not know because our system only uses Touch 'n Go," the user wrote.
Meanwhile, @AbirulKarim suggested broader payment options should be introduced.
"It is inconvenient to rely only on Touch 'n Go cards. There should be QR payment and debit card payment options," the user said.
Another user, @Beruskapal, said: "Before going up, ask first. There is nothing wrong. This is called the shame of asking astray."
Another user, @Juniverse, said: "Please do research before travelling."
The incident has reignited discussions on improving Malaysia's public transport experience for tourists, particularly as the country prepares for increased tourist arrivals under upcoming tourism campaigns.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Govt launches real-time dashboard to track global supply crisis
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: The public can now monitor developments related to the Global Supply Crisis through a new dashboard developed by the Department of Statistics Malaysia (DOSM) in collaboration with the Economy Ministry.
In a Facebook post today, the ministry said the Global Supply Crisis Monitoring Dashboard represents a new government approach to crisis management using near-real-time data.
It said the initiative aims to detect early signs of economic pressure, improve coordination of responses and ensure the public receives clearer and more accurate information.
The dashboard can be accessed via
pantaukrisis.gov.my
or through a QR code shared on the ministry's social media platforms.
However, checks by the New Straits Times found that the website could not be accessed at the time of writing, displaying an "ERR_CONNECTION_CLOSED" message.
Available data includes current fuel prices, petroleum trade performance, global energy prices, national economic indicators, currency exchange trends and commodity trade analytics.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: May 26 verdict on Hydroshoppe's corruption case transfer bid
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The High Court here today set May 26 to deliver its decision on an application by Hydroshoppe Sdn Bhd and its director to transfer a corruption case from the Sessions Court to the High Court.
The case involves an alleged offer of RM500,000 annually for 15 years to Tan Sri Annuar Musa in connection with the takeover of the Kuala Lumpur Tower (KL Tower) concession.
Judge Noor Ruwena Md Nurdin fixed the date after hearing submissions from Hydroshoppe and Datuk Abdul Hamid Shaikh Abdul Razak Shaikh's lawyer, Datuk Amer Hamzah Arshad, and deputy public prosecutor Farah Ezlin Yusop Khan.
Earlier, Amer Hamzah explained that the charges involve two separate entities: one against Abdul Hamid Shaikh as an individual under Section 16(b)(B) of the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) Act 2009 and another against the company under Section 17A(1)(a) of the same Act.
"Although separate, both are based on the same allegation, raising concerns over self-incrimination and potential double jeopardy. The issues raised are of such complexity and importance that they warrant determination by the High Court," he said.
The lawyer added that the charges in both proceedings raise complex questions of law, particularly regarding constitutional infringements under Articles 5, 7, and 8 of the Federal Constitution.
Farah Ezlin, however, argued that double jeopardy did not arise, as the charges involved different parties: Section 16 of the MACC Act 2009 was directed at Abdul Hamid Shaikh as an individual, while Section 17A of the same Act was directed at Hydroshoppe as a company.
"The offences may overlap factually, but they do not overlap legally. Each provision requires proof of an element that the other does not.
"Double jeopardy is only engaged where the accused is tried twice for the same offence in law. It is not engaged merely because two offences arise from the same transaction.
"Applying the same principle here, Section 16(b)(B) and Section 17A may arise from the same factual matrix, but they are not the same offence," she said, adding that the application is misconceived, premature and unsupported by any cogent evidence.
Previously, the Sessions Court fixed trial dates on June 5, July 20 to 22, Aug 28, Oct 5 to 7 and Oct 19 to 21, as well as Dec 1 to 4, 2026.
On April 5, 2023, Abdul Hamid, 52, was charged with offering a bribe through Datuk Tan Ser Lay to induce Annuar Musa, the then Minister of Communications and Multimedia, to expedite the handover of management and administration of the KL Tower concession to his company.
He was charged under Section 16(b)(B) of the MACC Act 2009, which provides for a maximum penalty of 20 years' imprisonment, or a fine of either five times the value of the bribe or RM10,000, whichever is higher.
The alleged offence took place at the Ministry of Communications and Multimedia in Putrajaya between July and August 2022.
Hydroshoppe is also charged under Section 17A(1)(a) of the same Act as a commercial organisation. The company is accused of jointly offering the bribe to Tan for Annuar's benefit at the same location and on the same dates.
Upon conviction, this offence carries a fine of at least 10 times the bribe amount or RM1 million, whichever is higher, or imprisonment of up to 20 years, or both.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Sepang cop died of heart disease during marching drill, post-mortem confirms
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A post-mortem conducted on a police corporal who collapsed during a marching drill at the Sepang district police headquarters on Wednesday confirmed that his death was caused by coronary atherosclerosis, or heart disease.
Sepang police chief Assistant Commissioner Norhizam Bahaman said the 37-year-old corporal, attached to the Dato' Abu Bakar Baginda police station, died during an additional marching exercise conducted at the headquarters parade ground at about 4pm on May 13.
He said the findings were obtained following a post-mortem conducted at Sultan Idris Shah Hospital in Serdang.
"The detailed explanation regarding the findings has been conveyed personally by the forensic pathology expert to the deceased's family members," he said in a statement today.
Norhizam also denied allegations circulating on social media claiming that the incident involved elements of abuse or foul play.
"Sepang police deny baseless allegations claiming the incident involved abuse.
"Nevertheless, a committee of inquiry has been established to carry out further investigations in accordance with standard operating procedures in a professional and thorough manner to ensure every aspect is fully scrutinised," he said.
He advised the public against spreading unverified speculation out of respect for the sensitivity of the deceased's family.
The deceased, identified earlier as Corporal Khairil Idzhar Rodzi, was reported to have collapsed while allegedly undergoing disciplinary punishment involving a "double march" exercise at the district police headquarters.
The 37-year-old was ordered to perform the punishment at about 2.30pm before he suddenly lost consciousness halfway through.
Cardiopulmonary resuscitation (CPR) was administered while waiting for an ambulance to arrive, but he was later pronounced dead by a medical officer.
Khairil was reportedly subjected to disciplinary action after allegedly being caught using his mobile phone while on duty, among other offences.
Khairil, who had served in the force for 17 years, was described as a great loss to the Selangor contingent.
He was laid to rest at the Masjid Mahmudiiah Islamic cemetery in Bukit Keteri, Perlis, at about 11am yesterday.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: India's Ola Electric to invest US$208.5mil in EV, cell tech units
Article:
BENGALURU: India's Ola Electric will invest US$208.5 million in its core vehicle and cell units as the EV bike and scooter maker aims to step up cost cuts and localise manufacturing to achieve profitability amid rising competition.
The SoftBank-backed firm has been grappling with higher operating costs and is seeking to bring them down through automation, job cuts and increasing in-house production of EV cells. The firm also plans to launch a new cost-efficient line of EV two-wheeler models.
The investment is expected to be completed by May 14, 2027, Ola Electric said in a statement.
Last year, the company started manufacturing its own battery cells instead of importing them, a move that it previously said is key to achieving profitability.
In February, it projected lower operating costs by as much as 50 per cent in the coming quarters, after posting a narrower third-quarter loss as it sets its sights on turning profitable. Ola is yet to report its March-quarter results.
Its EV unit posted a revenue of 47.17 billion rupees for the year ended March 31, 2026, while its cell unit posted a revenue of 730 million rupees.
The company, which once commanded half of India's e-scooter market, has lost ground to legacy players such as Bajaj Auto and TVS Motor, which widened distribution and rolled out competing models, as well as to rival Ather Energy.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: BYD to work with Malaysian govt on EV future
Article:
BYD Co Ltd has said that it remains committed to investing in Malaysia amid uncertainty of the Chinese carmaker's future after the government tightens rules on fully imported (CBU) electric vehicles.
BYD vice president Liu Xueliang said it will continue working closely with the government, distributors and dealer partners to develop new energy vehicle (NEV) solutions that are better suited to the local market.
Starting July 1 this year, all fully imported EVs entering Malaysia must meet a minimum cost, insurance and freight (CIF) value of RM200,000 and a minimum power output of 180kW.
The new requirements follow the expiry of Malaysia's special EV import exemption scheme at the end of 2025.
Some consumers and industry watchers argued the move may slow adoption of affordable EVs by pushing imported models into the luxury segment.
BYD had previously signalled long-term ambitions here, including plans linked to assembly operations in Tanjung Malim, Perak.
Earlier this year, the Investment, Trade and Industry Ministry clarified that BYD's proposed manufacturing licence was tied to export-oriented production, localisation requirements and pricing conditions that apply to all investors equally.
Liu, who is also general manager of BYD Asia Pacific auto sales division, said the
company respects all policies set by the government.
It hopes to support Malaysian consumers through its technology, operational expertise and long-term commitment to the market.
"Together with the relevant authorities of the Malaysian government and our distributor and dealer partners, we will find a path that is more suitable for the development of Malaysian automotive new energy vehicle solutions."
Liu spoke at the opening of the brand's third showroom in Penang by BYD and its distributor Sime Motors here today.
Liu also highlighted growing demand for EVs particularly during a recent visit to Sabah and Sarawak.
"Last year, I went to East Malaysia. I felt a lot of demands and different types of demands from the East Malaysian customers.
"I have made a promise to them that I will definitely bring their favourite new energy vehicle products to East Malaysia," he added.
Meanwhile, Liu said the latest Penang showroom is part of BYD's broader effort to contribute to Malaysia's green technology development and EV ecosystem.
The automaker believes Penang could play an important role in Malaysia's green mobility ambitions, supported by investments in EV-related infrastructure, technical training and aftersales capabilities.
Malaysia has been positioning itself as a regional EV hub through various incentives and industrial policies aimed at encouraging EV adoption, localisation and investment in charging infrastructure and talent development.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Kara Barista Coconut Milk shines at ICBS 2026
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Kara Malaysia reinforced its position in the local café industry at the International Café & Beverage Show (ICBS) 2026, taking on an expanded role as official barista coconut milk sponsor and on-ground branding partner.
Backed by more than 25 years of coconut expertise, Kara Malaysia showcased its "From Tree To Cup" philosophy, highlighting how its coconut milk is developed specifically for barista use.
The product is designed to perform consistently in café environments. This is done by sourcing selected coconuts and extending to controlled processing that preserves texture and flavour.
At the exhibition, the brand translated this approach into live demonstrations and hands-on sessions.
Baristas Tyler Wong and Brady Sak led demonstrations that focused on steaming techniques, texture control, and latte art application using coconut milk.
The sessions showed how plant-based milk behaves differently from dairy, requiring precision while offering stable foam and clean finishes when executed correctly.
Kara Barista Coconut Milk was also presented across a range of applications, from hot and iced coffee to tea-based beverages and desserts, reflecting its versatility in modern café menus.
Sampling sessions, including chilled drinks and a cocoa-based beverage known as Kara Choconut, provided visitors with a direct experience of its taste and texture.
The booth also served as a meeting point for café owners, baristas, and industry stakeholders, facilitating discussions on workflow efficiency, menu development, and the increasing demand for plant-based alternatives.
Kara Malaysia brand manager Jasmine Teoh said the event provided an opportunity to better understand industry needs while strengthening engagement with café professionals.
Meanwhile, Kara Marketing Malaysia director Susanto noted that the company remains focused on developing solutions aligned with evolving consumer preferences, particularly in the plant-based segment.
Kara Barista's expanded presence at ICBS 2026 reflects its growing role not just as a product supplier but as a partner within the café ecosystem, supporting baristas and businesses adapting to changing trends.
Learn more about Kara Barista
here
.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PM urges efficient delivery of 13MP projects for people's benefit
Article:
SEREMBAN: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has called for all projects under the 13th Malaysia Plan (13MP) to be implemented efficiently and closely monitored to ensure they genuinely benefit the people.
He said the matter was emphasised during the Seremban district development meeting today, which reviewed the implementation status of programmes and projects under the 13MP.
He said discussions focused on areas closely linked to the people's needs, including education, flood mitigation, infrastructure, healthcare and socio-economic strengthening.
"I stressed that every project and programme planned must truly benefit the people and be felt on the ground.
"As such, the Madani government will continue coordinating efforts across ministries and agencies to ensure more efficient implementation and faster delivery of assistance and development," he said in a Facebook post.
Anwar, who is also finance minister, said project monitoring must be strengthened to prevent delays, leakages or implementation issues that could affect the people.
"Every project must be monitored carefully and implemented with full responsibility and integrity.
"Our priority is to ensure every plan is translated into effective action for the wellbeing of the people and the progress of the nation," he said.
Earlier, Anwar attended the 2026
National Youth Day
celebration here today.
Also present were Youth and Sports Minister Dr Mohammed Taufiq Johari, Negri Sembilan Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun, Dewan Rakyat Speaker Tan Sri Johari Abdul, and Malaysian Youth Council president Mohd Izzat Afifi Abdul Hamid.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] Ex-driver charged with criminally intimidating High Court judge
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A former driver pleaded not guilty at the Magistrate's Court here today to a charge of criminally intimidating a High Court judge he once served.
Mohd Fareez Mohd Zin, 39, was accused of criminally intimidating High Court civil judge Gan Techiong, 64, with the intention of causing fear and distress.
The unemployed father of three entered his plea after the charge was read before magistrate Farah Nabihah Muhamad Dan.
The alleged offence took place at 1.30pm at Gan's court complex in Jalan Duta on May 8.
The charge, framed under Section 506 of the Penal Code, carries a jail term of up to two years, a fine, or both, upon conviction.
If the threat involves death or grievous hurt, the offence is punishable with up to seven years' imprisonment, a fine, or both.
Deputy public prosecutor Amritpreet Kaur Randhawa objected to bail, stressing that the offence is non-bailable.
"I object to any bail application in this case. The court must ensure the safety of judges in Malaysia.
"If bail is granted, the amount should be substantial and the accused must be barred from interfering with prosecution witnesses and ordered to report to the police station," she said.
Defence lawyer Karen Kaur urged the court to set bail at a minimal amount, stating that her client had undertaken to attend all future court proceedings.
"He is not a flight risk and the primary purpose of bail is to secure his attendance in court.
"The amount should take into account his financial circumstances and he has no objection to any additional conditions imposed by the court," she said.
After hearing submissions from both parties, Farah Nabihah denied bail, citing the non-bailable nature of the offence and the prosecution's objection.
"Given the nature of the charge, the court declines to grant bail at this stage.
"The bail application may be made before the original magistrate who will hear this case," she said.
Investigations revealed that on May 8, the accused allegedly sent two threatening WhatsApp voice notes and made a phone call to Gan's secretary.
In the voice notes, he allegedly made defamatory remarks and threats against the judge.
In a separate case, Fareez also pleaded not guilty to a charge of self-administering amphetamine and methamphetamine at 11.10pm on May 12 at the toilet of the Sentul district police headquarters Narcotics Investigation Department.
The charge, framed under Section 15(1)(a) of the Dangerous Drugs Act 1952, carries a maximum fine of RM5,000 or imprisonment of up to two years, as well as mandatory supervision for not less than two years, upon conviction.
Amritpreet requested a mention date pending submission of the pathology report and proposed bail at RM4,000 with one surety.
Karen sought a lower bail amount.
Farah Nabihah allowed bail at RM2,000 with one surety.
She set Aug 11 for mention of both cases.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Cops record Pas MP's statement over controversial ceramah remarks
Article:
KOTA BARU: Police today recorded a statement from Datuk Dr Nik Muhammad Zawawi Salleh following his controversial remarks made during a recent ceramah.
The Pasir Puteh member of parliament was summoned to the Kota Baru district police headquarters in Pengkalan Chepa this morning.
Kota Baru district police chief Assistant Commissioner Mohd Azmir Damiri confirmed the matter when contacted.
He said police had recorded Nik Zawawi's statement to facilitate investigations into the case.
Meanwhile, Nik Zawawi, in a Facebook post today, confirmed that he had given his statement to the police.
"Alhamdulillah, on this Friday morning, I came to the Kota Baru district police station to have my statement recorded regarding my remarks during a ceramah recently.
"I gave my full cooperation to the police. This issue has given me greater awareness to be more careful with facts from religious texts so that no problems arise and I remain safe.
"This incident has taught me a lesson," he said.
On Wednesday, the
Cabinet condemned remarks
allegedly made by Nik Zawawi that were deemed insulting towards the companions of Prophet Muhammad during a ceramah.
Unity government spokesman Datuk Seri Fahmi Fadzil said the matter was raised during the Cabinet meeting.
Fahmi said the Cabinet unanimously rejected the remarks and described them as unacceptable.
Earlier, several video clips went viral on social media showing Nik Zawawi delivering a ceramah in which he allegedly made remarks suggesting that four companions of the Prophet — Abu Bakar as-Siddiq, Umar al-Khattab, Uthman Affan and Ali Abi Talib — had disgraceful pasts before embracing Islam.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Palestine embassy urges global action, accountability on Nakba anniversary
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR:The Embassy of Palestine in Malaysia has urged the international community to uphold international law and ensure accountability over continuing violations against Palestinians.
In a statement issued in conjunction with the 78th anniversary of the Nakba, the embassy said the destruction unfolding in the occupied Palestinian territories was a pattern of violations in flagrant breach of international humanitarian law and binding international resolutions.
It also called for an end to the entrenched system of impunity and urged the international community to hold Israel fully accountable for its continuing crimes, including war crimes, crimes against humanity and genocide.
The embassy reiterated its absolute and categorical rejection of all policies of occupation, settlement expansion, annexation, forced displacement and ethnic cleansing.
The statement stressed that the issue of Palestinian refugees remains at the core of the Palestinian cause, while reaffirming that the right of return is a permanent legal, historical, collective and individual right that does not expire with time and cannot be nullified by force.
According to the embassy, the right is affirmed under United Nations General Assembly Resolution 194 and cannot be replaced, circumvented or dissolved through schemes of resettlement, forced displacement or so-called alternative solutions.
The embassy also defended the mandate of the United Nations Relief and Works Agency for Palestine Refugees in the Near East (UNRWA), describing it as a fundamental international obligation.
"Any attempt to undermine, weaken or terminate this mandate constitutes an assault on the international legal order itself and a direct attempt to erase the legal existence of Palestinian refugees," it said.
The statement further said that refugee camps such as Jenin, Nur Shams and Tulkarm are subjected to repeated invasions, systematic destruction and forced displacement.
It also reaffirmed support for "the establishment of an independent, fully sovereign Palestinian state on the June 4, 1967 borders, with East Jerusalem as its capital."
Nakba in Arabic means "catastrophe". It refers to the 1948 Palestinian exodus, in which around  700,000 Palestinians were displaced or fled during the conflict surrounding the creation of the state of Israel.
Nakba is observed annually on May 15, and it is a day of remembrance for Palestinian displacement and loss of land.
– BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 32 foreign women fined RM1,000 each over passport offences
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Thirty-two foreign women were fined RM1,000 each by the magistrate's court here today after pleading guilty to violating conditions related to their passports earlier this month.
Magistrate Atifah Hazimah Wahab imposed the maximum fine on all the accused, aged between 22 and 40.
Based on the charge sheets, they were accused of, without reasonable excuse, breaching conditions imposed on their passports at an entertainment outlet along Jalan Dato Abdullah Tahir in Kampung Wadihana here at about 2.30am on May 3.
They were charged under Regulation 39(6) of the Immigration Regulations 1963, which carries a jail term of up to six months, a fine of up to RM1,000, or both upon conviction.
The prosecution was conducted by deputy public prosecutor Nik Noratini Nik Azman, while all the accused were represented by lawyer Gary Ng Boon Hui.
Meanwhile, a local man believed to be the owner of the premises, who was supposed to be charged in the same court for allegedly allowing illegal immigrants to enter and stay at the premises, was absent.
He is expected to be charged under Section 55E of the Immigration Act 1959/63.
In the same court, two other foreign women claimed trial to overstaying in Malaysia without reasonable excuse.
The accused, identified as South Korean national Ham Minji, 35, and Thai national Saranpath Fueksanthia, 27, were alleged to have committed the offence at the same location, date and time.
They were charged under Section 15(1)(c) of the Immigration Act 1959/63 and are punishable under Section 15(4) of the same Act, which provides for a fine of up to RM10,000, imprisonment of up to five years, or both upon conviction.
The court allowed bail at RM7,000 each with two local sureties and fixed June 15 for case mention.
In separate proceedings before another magistrate's court, self-employed businessman Tan Soon Ee, 62, who is also believed to be the premises owner, was fined RM3,000 or four months' jail after pleading guilty to operating an entertainment outlet beyond permitted hours.
Magistrate Nabilah Nizam handed down the sentence after the local man admitted to the charge under Section 11 of the Johor Entertainment and Places of Entertainment Enactment 1998.
The offence carries a penalty under Section 11(2) of the same enactment of a fine not exceeding RM10,000, imprisonment of up to one year, or both upon conviction.
In the same court, 29 foreign women were also charged under Regulation 39(6) of the Immigration Regulations 1963 for breaching passport conditions without reasonable excuse.
Four other foreign women were charged under Section 15(1)(c) of the Immigration Act 1959/63 for overstaying, while another foreign woman was charged under Section 6(1)(c) of the same Act for not possessing valid travel documents.
At the same time, before magistrate A. Shaarmini, 41 foreign women were also charged under Regulation 39(6) of the Immigration Regulations 1963.
One of them was fined RM900 after pleading guilty to the charge.
The remaining accused did not enter any plea as they did not understand either Bahasa Malaysia or English.
The court fixed May 21 for a Thai interpreter and June 11 for a Vietnamese interpreter.
In the same court, three foreign women were charged under Section 15(1)(c) of the Immigration Act 1959/63, while another woman was charged under Section 6(1)(c) of the same Act.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Russia, Ukraine swap 205 prisoners of war each
Article:
KYIV: Russia and Ukraine exchanged 205 prisoners of war each today, Moscow and Kyiv said, a week after US President Donald Trump announced a large swap would take place between the warring sides.
The Russian defence ministry said in a statement on social media that "205 Russian servicemen were returned from territory" controlled by Kyiv, adding that, "in exchange, 205 Ukrainian armed forces prisoners of war were transferred."
Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said on Telegram most of the Ukrainians handed over had been in Russian captivity since 2022.
Trump said last week that Russia and Ukraine would carry out a mutual swap of 1,000 prisoners as he announced a three-day US-brokered ceasefire that covered Russia's May 9 parade celebrating the defeat of the Nazis.
Both sides have traded accusations of violating the truce and Ukraine has accused Moscow of ramping up its strikes against civilians after it expired, killing at least 24 in an air barrage on Kyiv on Thursday.
"This is the first phase of the 1,000-for-1,000 prisoner exchange," Zelensky said.
He posted pictures of the released Ukrainians, wrapped in national blue-and-yellow flags, smiling and embracing each other.
Zelenskyy said they included troops who fought in the bloody battle for Mariupol's steelworks Azovtsal and those who defended Chernobyl, which briefly fell to Moscow at the start of its invasion.
The POW swaps remain one of the few remaining areas of cooperation between the two sides, at war since Russia ordered troops into its neighbour in February 2022.
Moscow's defence ministry said its troops were brought to its ally Belarus, where "they are receiving the necessary psychological and medical assistance."
It added that "the United Arab Emirates provided humanitarian assistance during the return of the Russian servicemen from captivity." - AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] Applications for non-national stream graduates to open on June 30
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: Applications for students from outside the national education system to enter public higher education institutions will open on June 30.
Higher Education director-general Datuk Prof Dr Azlinda Azman said applications would first be handled directly by the respective universities before being made available through the UPUOnline system next year.
"UPUOnline applications have already been in progress, with preparations having started at the end of last year.
"In line with the Cabinet's decision, universities have held several engagement sessions and, insya-Allah, we will open direct applications to universities offering the relevant programmes from June 30," she said at a press conference at the ministry's headquarters today.
She said the direct application method was to ensure the existing UPUOnline process would not be disrupted.
She said the universities involved in the identified programmes, including those related to tahfiz leavers, included Universiti Sultan Zainal Abidin, Universiti Pendidikan Sultan Idris (UPSI), Universiti Sains Islam Malaysia and Universiti Teknologi Mara.
For Chinese language-related degree programmes, Azlinda said four programmes were currently available in three universities.
They are the Bachelor of Arts in Chinese Language Studies with Honours at Universiti Putra Malaysia, the Bachelor of Chinese Language and Linguistics and Bachelor of Arts in Chinese Studies at Universiti Malaya, and the Bachelor of Chinese Language with Education at UPSI.
Yesterday, the Higher Education Ministry announced that the Cabinet had approved new public university admission pathways for students from outside the national education system.
Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the
government was refining details of its decision to allow school leavers
from those institutions to pursue selected programmes at public universities.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Entry standards for non-national stream leavers cannot be lowered, says ministry
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: Entry standards and quality for school leavers from outside the national education system into public higher education institutions cannot be compromised, the Higher Education Ministry said today.
Higher Education director-general Datuk Prof Dr Azlinda Azman said admission requirements would remain under the authority of each university senate, which determines academic standards and quality.
"At the university level, we already have our own requirements. We have studied this, and for the ministry, the university senate is the highest authority.
"They determine the quality and standard of admission. This is something we cannot compromise," she said at a press conference at the ministry's headquarters today.
Her remarks came after the United
Chinese School Committees' Association of Malaysia
(Dong Zong) criticised the government's new conditional pathway for students outside the national education system to enter public universities, saying the admission requirements remained restrictive.
She said candidates from UEC and tahfiz streams must still possess qualifications from their respective institutions, as well as a full Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) certificate with at least a credit in Bahasa Melayu and a pass in History.
"The importance of Bahasa Melayu and History is placed at the highest level because they form the foundation of nationhood, and there is no compromise on both subjects," she said.
She said UEC holders with a full SPM certificate could still apply for any programme through the normal UPU route, including critical courses such as engineering and medicine, based on their SPM results.
Azlinda added that students admitted through the pathway would be considered part of the mainstream public university system and remain eligible for government subsidies and National Higher Education Fund Corporation (PTPTN) loans.
"They may apply for any programme, subject to their results. There is no compromise, no discrimination and no bias.
"They apply through UPUOnline based on the merit of their SPM results," she added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Ministry probes petrol station over alleged RON95 sale to foreign-registered vehicle
Article:
BERUAS: The Perak Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry has launched an investigation into a petrol station here following allegations that a foreign-registered vehicle attempted to purchase subsidised RON95 petrol using deceptive tactics to evade detection.
Its director Datuk Kamalludin Ismail said the action was taken after complaints were lodged and information regarding the incident went viral on social media.
He said initial investigations revealed that the incident occurred at 9pm on Wednesday.
"KPDN found that the RON95 purchase was made by a Malaysian citizen through the BUDI95 programme, with payment made using a credit card at the station counter, while the vehicle involved was suspected of bearing foreign registration plates.
"Checks on the transaction found that only part of the fuel purchase was successfully dispensed before the refuelling process was halted entirely," he said in a statement today.
He said closed-circuit television (CCTV) footage from the petrol station in Ayer Tawar had been obtained and reviewed to assist in the investigation.
He said an investigation paper had been opened under Regulation 3(2) of the Supply Control Regulations (Prohibition on the Sale and Purchase of Controlled Goods) (RON95 Petrol) 2026 for allegedly breaching Regulation 3(1), which prohibits the sale of RON95 petrol for use in foreign-registered vehicles without written permission from the Controller.
Another investigation is also being conducted under Regulation 4(3) of the same regulations for allegedly breaching Regulation 4(1), which prohibits the purchase of RON95 petrol for use in foreign-registered vehicles without written approval.
The case is being investigated under the Control of Supplies Act 1961.
Upon conviction, individuals may face fines of up to RM1 million for a first offence and up to RM3 million for subsequent offences, or imprisonment of up to three years, or both.
Companies convicted of the offence may be fined up to RM2 million for a first offence and up to RM5 million for subsequent offences.
Kamalludin urged the public to act as the eyes and ears of the authorities by cooperating and providing any information they may have, especially regarding the misuse of controlled and subsidised goods.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Govt focus on reforms strengthens Malaysia's economic momentum – Anwar
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The government's focus on MADANI Economy reforms, anchored on pro-growth policies, people-centric programmes and responsible fiscal management, has continued to strengthen Malaysia's economic momentum, said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim.
Anwar, who is also the Finance Minister, said this approach has helped Malaysia build on the 5.2 per cent growth recorded in 2024 and 2025 to achieve 5.4 per cent growth in the first quarter (1Q) of 2026.
"At the same time, responsible fiscal management has enabled the government to cushion the impact of higher global fuel prices on the people and businesses through measures such as Budi Madani RON95 (BUDI95) and enhanced assistance under BUDI Diesel, while continuing to support household spending and economic growth," Anwar said in a statement released by the Finance Ministry (MoF).
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Remand extended for drink driver over wrong-way crash, rider in ICU
Article:
KLANG: The remand of a suspected drink driver who allegedly drove against traffic before crashing into a motorcycle along Persiaran Kota, leaving the rider critically injured, has been extended by four days until next Tuesday.
Klang South district police chief Assistant Commissioner Lim Jit Huey, when contacted, said the victim, a man in his 20s, remained in critical condition at the intensive care unit (ICU) of Tengku Ampuan Rahimah Hospital (HTAR).
"Yes, the remand has been extended for four days until next Tuesday, May 19," he said.
He added that the suspect, in his 30s, had no prior criminal or drug records.
It was previously reported that the
motorcyclist was hit by a car
driven by a man believed to be intoxicated and travelling against traffic at 12.30am.
The victim was thrown from his motorcycle upon impact, suffering severe injuries to his body and right leg.
Initial investigations found the suspect had been driving from Port Klang towards Shah Alam before allegedly veering into the opposite lane and travelling against traffic for about 3.5km.
Screening tests confirmed the driver was positive for alcohol.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] MOHE: Public university access move not recognition of other education systems
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: The Higher Education Ministry has stressed that the government's move to allow school leavers from outside the national education system to apply to public universities should not be viewed as recognition of other education systems.
Its director-general Datuk Professor Dr Azlinda Azman said the move was aimed at widening access to education, not recognising any external system.
"All sorts of parties have given their views, but for us, this is an opportunity to open access and not to recognise.
"We at the ministry, as I said earlier, are inclusive. Our position on access to education is clear, and under the Malaysia Higher Education Plan 2026-2035, we want to walk the talk," she said at a press conference at the ministry's headquarters here today.
Yesterday, the Higher Education Ministry announced that cabinet had
approved new public university admission pathways
for students from outside the national education system which includes tahfiz, international school and Unified Examinations Certificate (UEC) leavers.
Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abd Kadir said the
move did not compromise the national education system
, as it was aimed at widening access, not recognising other education systems within the national framework.
Asked whether there were concerns that the inclusion of tahfiz leavers was used as the basis for UEC admission, Azlinda said the matter should be viewed in terms of access, not recognition.
On whether UEC and Sijil Tinggi Persekolahan Malaysia (STPM) qualifications would be treated similarly under the merit system, Azlinda said both qualifications were different.
"No, actually there is a big difference between UEC qualification and STPM qualification. Once again, this is not about recognition. There is no recognition of the UEC syllabus.
"What is important is that we are providing the pathway. In the selection process, universities will assess students based on their respective conditions and criteria.
"To say which is higher or lower is also not fair. We will have to go through the process once applications are made," she added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: BYD, Sime Motors expand northern presence with Penang flagship hub
Article:
BYD Malaysia and Sime Motors, the local distributor of BYD vehicles, are expanding showrooms, aftersales support and training programmes as they strengthen the electric vehicle (EV) ownership experience natuonwide.
The companies currently operate more than 43 showrooms and over 25 dedicated aftersales centres, while also investing in local talent development and service capabilities to support the growing number of EV owners.
The latest development is the opening of BYD Mansion Macalister in George Town, Penang, a restored heritage property that now serves as the brand's northern flagship showroom.
The launch on Friday was attended by Penang Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow and state executive councillor H'ng Mooi Lye.
The event also marked the milestone of BYD reaching the 5,000th owner in the northern region.
Liu Xueliang, vice president of BYD Co Ltd and general manager of BYD Asia Pacific auto sales division, said the company sees long-term potential in Malaysia's EV market.
"Our commitment to Malaysia reflects BYD's global vision of making sustainable mobility accessible to all.
"We continue to channel our technological innovations and research capabilities into this market because we believe in Malaysia's electric future."
He added that the company's investment in training local talent and introducing new EV technologies demonstrates its long-term plans for the country.
"BYD Mansion Macalister serves as a premier embodiment of this vision, seamlessly blending world-class innovation with a deep respect for local heritage," he said.
Jeffrey Gan, managing director of Southeast Asia at Sime Motors, said customer support remains a key focus as EV adoption grows.
"Our commitment to Malaysia is measured in the trust our customers place in us every day.
"We are building a local network that ensures every BYD owner enjoys seamless, reassuring support for the lifetime of their vehicle."
He said the company now operates more than 30 aftersales centres supported by certified local technicians and service advisors.
Located in a restored landmark building, BYD Mansion Macalister includes consultation lounges, digital touchpoints, customer areas, an integrated service centre and charging facilities aimed at improving convenience for EV owners in the northern region.
The companies are also continuing training programmes for technicians, sales advisors and aftersales personnel to strengthen local EV expertise.
The company said customers are also supported by a 95 per cent aftersales parts fill rate, aimed at improving service turnaround times and ownership experience.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Education Ministry fills 50 school leadership posts, says vacancies will be addressed promptly
Article:
IPOH, May 15 — The Ministry of Education has filled 50 vacant posts for headmasters and principals nationwide as part of efforts to strengthen school leadership and ensure smooth school administration.
Its Minister, Fadhlina Sidek, said the ministry views the issue seriously, stressing that schools cannot function effectively without strong leadership.
She said strong leadership is crucial in implementing school reforms, ensuring safety, and managing daily school operations.
“Schools would not be able to build a proper ecosystem without strong leadership, making it crucial for the ministry to take swift action in filling vacant positions.
“Therefore, the ministry takes this matter seriously and has taken immediate steps to fill these vacancies so that issues, including safety concerns and others, can be addressed promptly,” she said.
She said this at a press conference after attending the 55th national-level Teachers’ Day Awards ceremony held at the Kinta Riverfront Hotel here.
She said the appointments involved schools in every state and took effect immediately.
“Today, we have completed the appointments of headmasters and principals for schools in every state. These appointments take effect starting today, meaning they can continue with their administrative duties at their respective schools,” she said.
Fadhlina said that while about 50 posts were initially vacant, the ministry would continue to monitor the situation and fill any remaining gaps promptly.
“There are still vacancies, and we will ensure they are filled from time to time as quickly as possible,” she said.
She also said that the ministry had instructed senior officials to urgently review and resolve the matter, describing it as a priority requiring immediate attention.
“Therefore, I have asked the ministry’s director-general and chief secretary to review our commitment to resolving these issues quickly,” she said.

Title: MACC clears Krishnan Tan of wrongdoing, IJM chairman says he wants to move on
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, May 15 — Tan Sri Krishnan Tan today confirmed that the Malaysian Anti‑Corruption Commission (MACC) has formally cleared him of wrongdoing, following the conclusion of investigations into the sale of IJM Corporation Berhad shares to Sunway Berhad.
In a letter dated April 30, 2026, MACC informed him that the investigation had been completed and referred to the Deputy Public Prosecutor, who decided no charges would be brought. The case was categorised as No Further Action (NFA).
In a statement today, the IJM Corporation Berhad chairman said he welcomed the decision, adding that he had “steadfastly maintained” his innocence.
He said the investigation and related publicity had caused severe reputational damage and had placed immense strain on his family, friends and the organisations he serves.
Krishnan also expressed gratitude to those who stood by him, especially his wife, family and close friends.
“I now want to put this difficult period behind me and move on,” he said.
Previously, IJM Corporation Berhad had clarified that it is not being investigated as a corporate entity, and that MACC’s probe was confined to several individuals linked to the group.
IJM said appropriate measures were taken to ensure that the group’s business is conducted with integrity and free of corruption, adding that it has established adequate procedures and maintains robust governance frameworks, including the implementation of an Anti-Bribery Management System certified to ISO 37001:2025.
It also said the board and management team remain focused on business continuity, maintaining confidence among customers and partners, and upholding strong corporate governance and compliance standards.
The investigation first came to light in January 2026, when MACC officers reportedly visited IJM’s offices to obtain information relating to an alleged RM2.5 billion money laundering probe, which had earlier sparked speculation affecting the group’s shares and a high-profile takeover bid.

Title: Homeowner, swimming club in Ipoh settle pickleball noise row with indoor facility plan
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, May 15 — A homeowner and the Ipoh Swimming Club have reached a settlement in a lawsuit over noise from an outdoor pickleball court.
The Ipoh High Court recorded a consent judgment before judge Moses Susayan, with the club agreeing that no open‑air pickleball games would be allowed in the area adjoining the house, as reported by
Free Malaysia Today.
According to the judgment, the club will propose and proceed with the construction of an indoor facility at the designated site, subject to approvals from relevant authorities including the environment department.
“In the event the defendant breaches any of the above terms by allowing open‑air pickleball play, the plaintiff, Cheang Phoy Ken, is at liberty to initiate fresh legal proceedings,” the judgment stated.
Susayan ordered both parties to bear their own legal costs.
Cheang had filed the suit earlier this year, claiming the club built a pickleball court next to his home on Lorong Gopeng at Taman Golf.
He said members played daily between 7am and 10pm, with the court fully booked, and the noise created a “continuous nuisance.”
Cheang alleged the disturbance caused physical discomfort, mental stress and emotional distress, and he sought damages for loss of amenity, anxiety and medical expenses.
Pending the case, he applied for an interim injunction to stop the club from allowing open‑air pickleball games.
Lawyer S. Raam Kumar represented Cheang, while counsel N. Purshotamdas appeared for the club.

Title: Ex-polytechnic student walks free after judge bins 3.4kg drug trafficking charge, says key witness not credible
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, May 15 — The High Court today acquitted and discharged a former polytechnic student of a charge of trafficking 3,404 grammes (3.4 kilogrammes) of cannabis two years ago.
Judge Datuk Azhar Abdul Hamid made the ruling after finding that the prosecution had failed to establish a prima facie case against Harith Izzuddin Mohd Hanizam, 23, at the end of its case.
In delivering his decision, Azhar said the court found that the prosecution had relied on the testimony of the fourth prosecution witness (SP4), the complainant who is also a police officer, to prove the element of possession.
“The witness’ testimony is doubtful as he did not tell the truth about what happened after the accused and the case items (drugs) were brought to the police station after the arrest.
“The court found that SP4 was not a credible witness. I therefore ruled that the prosecution had failed to prove the element of possession,” he said, adding that no direct evidence was presented to establish that trafficking had occurred.
Azhar said SP4 had claimed that he was always at the police station during the interrogation of the accused, but the testimony of SP5, the investigating officer in the police report submitted by the defence, proved otherwise.
“Therefore, I ruled that the prosecution had failed to prove a prima facie case against the accused. With this, the accused is discharged and acquitted of the charge,” he said.
Five prosecution witnesses were called to testify in the trial, which began last Wednesday (May 13).
Harith Izzuddin was charged with trafficking the drugs at the lobby of a hotel along Jalan Kuching Batu Caves in Sentul here on Feb 26, 2024.
The charge was framed under Section 39B(1)(a) of the Dangerous Drugs Act 1952 and is punishable under Section 39B(2) of the same Act, which provides for the death penalty or life imprisonment, and whipping of not less than 12 strokes upon conviction.
Deputy Public Prosecutor Wan Ahmad Hijrah Wan Abdullah appeared for the prosecution, while Harith Izzuddin was represented by counsel Datuk Geethan Ram Vincent. — Bernama

Title: MACC investigates alleged multi-million ringgit bill of lading discrepancies at Tanjung Langsat Port in Johor
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, May 15 — The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) is investigating alleged discrepancies between bills of lading (BL) documentation and the actual physical movement of cargo within the petroleum supply chain at Tanjung Langsat Port in Johor.
Sources said the probe centres on alleged inconsistencies involving shipping documentation and cargo handling linked to transactions amounting to several million ringgit.
According to sources, a businessman bearing the title “Datuk Seri” met MACC officers at a hotel in Putrajaya on Monday at about 10.30am in relation to the complaint.
Further investigations by the relevant authorities are ongoing.
The development comes amid growing concerns within the maritime and commodity trading industry over mismatches between shipping documentation and actual cargo movement.
On May 13, Maritime Network Sdn Bhd chief executive officer Datuk Seri Jeyenderan Ramasamy warned that discrepancies involving bills of lading and physical cargo movement were emerging as a growing operational and regulatory risk in the global shipping and commodity trading sector.
The maritime industry veteran, who has more than 30 years of experience, said many outside the industry underestimated the importance of the BL, despite its central role in banking transactions, insurance arrangements, Customs declarations, cargo ownership representation and sanctions compliance.
“The biggest risk for a shipping agent is when the physical cargo movement and the documentation stop aligning. Any mismatch between operational handling and paperwork could expose all parties in the chain, including shipping agents, to significant risks,” he said in a statement.
Jeyenderan said shipping agents may not own the cargo or be the beneficial parties involved in transactions, but they still face operational exposure because authorities, terminals and counterparties rely heavily on the instructions and declarations processed through them.
As a result, he said shipping agents are now placing greater emphasis on documentation integrity, internal compliance controls and know-your-customer (KYC) verification processes.
He said the issue becomes more complicated in complex oil trades, where shipping agents may receive different instructions from traders, receivers, terminals, forwarding agents and logistics providers that do not fully match the original BL.
“The moment different parties start operating from different versions of the cargo story, the risk level immediately increases because the agent still has to ensure consistency from a regulatory and operational standpoint,” he said.
On oil blending and commingling after discharge, Jeyenderan said such practices are not unusual in the industry, but maintaining traceability becomes increasingly challenging once cargo enters storage systems and mixes with existing inventory.
He said if cargo conditions change operationally through transfers, blending or additional handling, the documentation and classification process must also evolve accordingly to ensure proper regulatory and commercial treatment.
Jeyenderan warned that when Customs records, BL documents, tank measurements and actual cargo positions no longer align, the industry risks losing visibility over cargo movement, potentially leading to compliance gaps, commercial disputes and revenue leakages.
He added that post-discharge cargo handling involving multiple tank transfers and storage movements is one area requiring closer global scrutiny, particularly where documentary treatment may not keep pace with physical cargo changes, potentially affecting tax and regulatory mechanisms.

Title: Home minister says drug problem in Langkawi real, but not like Pablo Escobar’s cartel
Article:
KULIM, May 15 — Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail has denied a news report that likened drug trafficking activities in Langkawi to the operations of an international cartel.
According to him, the magnitude of drug trafficking on the resort island cannot be likened to the drug cartel led by drug trafficker Pablo Escobar who controls the entire drug trafficking complex.
“In Langkawi, it’s not that big of a deal, but that’s okay, the media (Harian Metro) chose to say that, that’s okay, that’s their choice.
“We just know, we act on all the intelligence information we have and our spying is not just after the media reveals it, no, it’s spying all the time and when the time comes, we will take action to raid, then we do it,” he said.
He told reporters after the ceremony on Instilling Noble Values at his Community Service Centre in Taman Sejahtera, Lunas here today.
He said that the investigation into drug trafficking activities in Langkawi was carried out comprehensively by focusing on all elements including organised crimes and allegations of involvement of law enforcement officers.
Saifuddin Nasution said that the police would not protect any individuals including members who were involved in the activity and would open investigation papers according to existing laws.
Commenting on the proposal of Kedah Menteri Besar, Datuk Seri Muhammad Sanusi Md Nor for the police to shoot drug dealer, Saifuddin Nasution said the drug issue needs to be addressed rationally.
He also explained that Kedah is among the states with the most serious drug problems, especially involving cases under Section 39C of the Dangerous Drugs Act 1952, which is a repeat offence.
“So we have to sit calmly, with a cooler head, rationally and objectively. It is not as easy as saying ‘shoot’, that is to be popular, but it is not certain that it will solve the problem,” he said. — Bernama

Title: From service to second career: WBL programme helps military veterans build future prospects
Article:
MASJID TANAH, May 15 — The Defence Ministry is expanding its Work-Based Learning (WBL) programme to help military personnel secure better-paying and more stable jobs after retirement.
Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said many Armed Forces personnel enter service with only the Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) qualification and, without intervention, retire with the same academic credentials despite accumulating decades of skills and experience during their service.
“Most military personnel join the Armed Forces with only an SPM qualification.
“If without intervention, they leave service with the same qualification, making it difficult for them to secure better-paying jobs, especially in today’s competitive job market,” Khaled said after officiating the second phase of the WBL programme here today.
He said some veterans may have secured jobs but often end up with low wages, causing many to rely solely on their pensions while supporting growing families.
“Many leave or retire from service at the age of 41.
“If this issue is not addressed early, it will lead to various socio-economic problems among veterans, including exposure to poverty,” he added.
He stressed that military personnel typically spend around 20 years in service, during which they accumulate valuable skills, experience and technical expertise.
However, he said these competencies are often not formally recognised by employers because they lack accredited certification.
“To address this, the Defence Ministry has collaborated with higher education institutions through WBL programmes, where experience and skills gained during service are assessed and given due academic recognition,” he said.
Under the programme, universities evaluate a participant’s years of service, experience and competencies before awarding academic qualifications.
Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin visiting a Universiti Pertahanan Nasional Malaysia (UPNM) booth, accompanied by National Defence University Malaysia Vice-Chancellor Datuk Seri Arman Rumaizi (left), after the launch of the Work-Based Learning 2.0 programme at Terendak Camp, Sungai Udang, Melaka.
Khaled said the programme is fully funded by the government and participation is voluntary.
He said the pilot phase initially received RM3 million in funding, but the allocation has now been increased to RM10 million this year, enabling about 1,520 military personnel annually to participate.
Around 5,000 Armed Forces personnel retire each year, with most retiring at the age of 41, while senior-ranking officers typically retire at 55.
